metrics.name: visionOS
metrics.question: Will Apple announce visionOS 27 (or a successor major version to visionOS 26) at WWDC 2026?

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

simulation_result.question: Will Apple announce visionOS 27 (or a successor major version to visionOS 26) at WWDC 2026?
limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.875 (counted n=3); simulation-conditional 0.0 (resolved mass 1.00); headline 0.875 via combiner_unavailable_range_only. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the simulation-conditional forecast; the grounded prior is reported separately; this is a stated reason Lean V2 must not become the default yet

blueprint.causal_thesis: Apple's executive leadership, particularly the Software Engineering VP and the Vision Products Group lead, hold discretion over visionOS release plans. Despite reports of platform abandonment, the institutional inertia of WWDC OS announcements and the need to support existing Vision Pro hardware may

dropped_grounded_rates.quantity: Probability of platform abandonment given reported actions
dropped_grounded_rates.why: basis_quote not found verbatim in evidence

pruned.reason: no action, trigger, target or observation channel reaches a surviving terminal pathway (evidence-mention-only / non-causal)

apple_os_naming_transition.claim: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major visionOS release was called visionOS 26.

provenance.definition: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major visionOS release was called visionOS 26.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At WWDC 2025, Apple announced visionOS 26 as the successor to visionOS 2, adopting year-based naming.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro, introducing a new year-based naming convention.

vision_pro_sales_decline.claim: Sales of Apple Vision Pro have been low, with the M5 refresh failing to revive interest, and reports indicate Apple has effectively abandoned the platform.

provenance.definition: Sales of Apple Vision Pro have been low, with the M5 refresh failing to revive interest, and reports indicate Apple has effectively abandoned the platform.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: MacRumors reported that the M5 Vision Pro refresh failed to revive consumer interest, with total sales reaching only approximately 500,000 units.
cases.basis_quote: The M5 refresh of Apple Vision Pro failed to revive consumer interest, with total sales reaching only approximately 500,000 units.

cases.description: Apple stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team to other projects, cancelled Vision Air, and shifted focus to AI-powered smart glasses.
cases.basis_quote: Apple has reportedly stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team to other projects, cancelled the Vision Air project, and shifted focus toward AI-powered smart glasses.

wwdc_annual_os_announcement_pattern.claim: Apple has historically announced major new versions of its operating systems at WWDC each June.

provenance.definition: Apple has historically announced major new versions of its operating systems at WWDC each June.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At WWDC 2025, Apple announced visionOS 26, iOS 26, macOS 26, etc.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, iOS 26, macOS 26, watchOS 12, and tvOS 19 at WWDC 2025.

cases.description: At WWDC 2024, Apple announced visionOS 2, iOS 18, macOS 15, etc.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 2, iOS 18, macOS 15, watchOS 11, and tvOS 18 at WWDC 2024.

cases.description: At WWDC 2023, Apple announced visionOS 1, iOS 17, macOS 14, etc.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS, the first operating system for Apple Vision Pro, along with iOS 17, macOS 14, watchOS 10, and tvOS 17 at WWDC 2023.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: Apple announces a new major version of visionOS (successor to visionOS 26) at WWDC 2026

provenance.definition: A major version announcement of visionOS, either during the WWDC 2026 keynote or via an Apple Newsroom press release, between May 6, 2026 and June 12, 2026.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any announcement that names a new major version (e.g., visionOS 27, visionOS 3) intended to succeed visionOS 26.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Minor point updates (e.g., visionOS 26.5, 26.6) do not qualify.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Apple announced visionOS 26 at WWDC 2025 keynote and via Newsroom press release.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

cases.description: Apple announced visionOS 2 at WWDC 2024 keynote and via Newsroom press release.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 2, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

cases.description: Apple announced visionOS 1 at WWDC 2023 keynote and via Newsroom press release.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS, the first operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

apple_wwdc_keynote.quorum: CEO and VP of Software Engineering must be present
apple_wwdc_keynote.consequence_of_nonparticipation: No announcement would be made, resolving to No.

apple_newsroom.quorum: Marketing team must issue press release
apple_newsroom.consequence_of_nonparticipation: No press release would be issued, resolving to No.

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

craig_federighi.claim: Craig Federighi believes that despite weak Vision Pro sales, Apple must maintain the annual OS release cadence for visionOS to preserve developer trust and the brand's reputation for innovation. He sees visionOS 27 as a strategic necessity to demonstrate long-term commitment, even if hardware sales
beliefs: Annual major OS updates are essential to Apple's ecosystem credibility.
visionOS 27 can be delivered with existing engineering resources.
Abandoning the visionOS update cycle would signal platform death.
goals: Deliver visionOS 27 at WWDC 2026 as planned.
Maintain developer engagement with the visionOS platform.
Align visionOS 27 with iOS 27 and macOS 27 announcements.
commitments: Apple's historical pattern of annual OS updates at WWDC.
Internal roadmap likely set before team redistribution.
craig_federighi.pressures: Internal pressure from marketing to show continued investment; external pressure from developers who invested in visionOS.

relationships.mike rockwell: Former Vision Pro chief now leading Siri; may advocate for redirecting resources.
relationships.tim cook: Defers to Federighi on software release decisions.

stances: Pro-annual release cycle for all Apple OSes.
Believes software can outpace hardware adoption.
historical_case_refs: Apple continued macOS updates during Mac sales slump (2013-2015).
watchOS updates persisted despite low Series 0 sales.
distinguishing_observations: Federighi has not publicly signaled any deviation from the annual OS cycle.
visionOS 26.5 RC was released May 4, 2026, indicating active development.
craig_federighi.action_if_state: Federighi will ensure visionOS 27 is featured in the WWDC 2026 keynote, with demo and release notes, and will personally present it.
assumptions: Engineering team still has capacity to produce a major release.
transition_triggers: Direct order from Tim Cook to cancel visionOS 27.
Major developer exodus from visionOS platform.

craig_federighi.claim: Craig Federighi privately concludes that visionOS 27 is not viable due to the effective abandonment of Vision Pro hardware, team redistribution, and lack of developer interest. He decides to skip the major version update and instead release only minor updates to visionOS 26, or quietly discontinue t
beliefs: Annual major OS updates are wasteful if hardware platform is dead.
Developer trust is already lost; a token update won't restore it.
Resources are better spent on iOS 27 and macOS 27.
goals: Avoid embarrassment of announcing an OS for a dead platform.
Redirect engineering resources to higher-impact projects.
Minimize negative press about Vision Pro failure.
commitments: No public commitment to visionOS 27 exists.
Team redistribution to Siri (Mike Rockwell) reduces available headcount.
craig_federighi.pressures: Pressure from hardware division to not highlight a failed product; pressure from finance to cut costs.

relationships.mike rockwell: Now leads Siri; likely supports redirecting visionOS engineers to Siri.
relationships.tim cook: Cook may prioritize profitability over tradition.

stances: Willing to break historical pattern if platform is dead.
historical_case_refs: Apple discontinued AirPort line without a final OS update.
Apple stopped major updates to Aperture after hardware shift.
distinguishing_observations: No official confirmation of visionOS 27 development.
visionOS 26.5 RC is a minor update, not a major version.
craig_federighi.action_if_state: Federighi will not include visionOS 27 in the WWDC 2026 keynote; instead, he may mention continued support for visionOS 26 with minor updates, or omit visionOS entirely.
assumptions: Executive team agrees to break the annual OS cycle.
No major developer outcry forces a reversal.
transition_triggers: Tim Cook mandates a visionOS 27 announcement for PR reasons.
Leak of visionOS 27 features forces public commitment.

craig_federighi.claim: Craig Federighi decides to announce a major visionOS update but under a new name (e.g., 'visionOS 3' or 'visionOS X') to distance from the failed Vision Pro brand, while still fulfilling the annual OS update promise. This is a compromise between abandoning the platform and maintaining the release ca
beliefs: The 'visionOS' name is tainted by Vision Pro failure.
A rebranded OS can attract new developers for future smart glasses.
Annual OS cycle must be maintained, but branding can change.
goals: Deliver a major OS update without associating it with Vision Pro.
Position the new OS for future AR/glasses hardware.
Avoid breaking the WWDC announcement tradition.
commitments: Apple's naming transition to year-based OS versions is flexible.
No rule says visionOS must keep its name.
craig_federighi.pressures: Pressure from marketing to rebrand; pressure from developers to show progress.

relationships.mike rockwell: Rockwell's smart glasses project may benefit from a renamed OS.
relationships.tim cook: Cook may approve rebranding to salvage the software investment.

stances: Willing to innovate on naming to manage perception.
historical_case_refs: Apple renamed 'OS X' to 'macOS' to align with iOS naming.
distinguishing_observations: Apple has not officially stated visionOS naming is permanent.
Rumors of 'visionOS 3' naming have circulated in developer forums.
craig_federighi.action_if_state: Federighi will announce a major OS update at WWDC 2026 under a new name (e.g., 'visionOS 3' or 'Apple Vision OS'), with features clearly distinct from visionOS 26, and will emphasize its role in futur
assumptions: Rebranding is technically feasible without delaying release.
transition_triggers: Negative focus group feedback on 'visionOS' name.
Leak of smart glasses project forces early rebranding.

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether Tim Cook will fully support a rebranding without seeing concrete developer traction first.
deliberations.note: I've been waiting for Cook's signal, but the keynote is looming and the 'visionOS' name is a dead weight. Rockwell's glasses need a clean slate, and developers need a story that isn't 'Vision Pro 2.0'

include_in_keynote.description: Craig Federighi decides to include visionOS 27 in the WWDC keynote.
authority_required: Decides visionOS major version content and timing

issue_press_release.description: Apple PR issues a Newsroom press release announcing visionOS 27.
authority_required: Decides visionOS major version content and timing

novel_cast_vote.description: Craig Federighi casts a vote in an institutional process to secure approval for rebranding visionOS to xrOS.
